patch.value: pm-search-advanced: register a built-in SearchProvider exemplar (search capability)

pm-search-advanced is the natural owner of registerSearchProvider but only uses commands+schema; the search capability has zero first-party exemplar for authors building custom embeddings/vector stores.
Manifest gains search capability; activate() registers an Ollama/local SearchProvider (and optionally a vector-store adapter); assertion-helper test; SDK.md search-provider example references it.

pm-search-advanced is the natural owner of registerSearchProvider but only uses commands+schema; the search capability has zero first-party exemplar for authors building custom embeddings/vector stores.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Unblocked + ready: pm-kfd8 (closed this PR) shipped assertRegisteredSearchProvider in src/sdk/testing.ts (matches on definition.name over registrations.search_providers). When this item teaches pm-search-advanced to api.registerSearchProvider(...), its package spec should assert via assertRegisteredSearchProvider for a non-synthetic first-party exemplar. Finding: pm-search-advanced currently only registers commands+flags, not a SearchProvider — this item is the real search-capability exemplar.
